title: The Best Way to Store Your Wooden Spurtle for Sourdough Mixing
meta_description: Learn the best way to store your wooden spurtle for sourdough mixing to keep it durable and ready for use. Discover practical tips for longevity.
html:
Wooden spurtles are indispensable for sourdough mixing, but improper storage can shorten their lifespan and affect your starter’s health. Whether you’re using a wooden spurtle and sourdough starter stirrer set or a single tool, proper care ensures it stays warp-free, hygienic, and ready for your next batch. Let’s break down how to store your wooden spurtle for sourdough mixing like a pro.

Why Proper Storage Matters for Wooden Spurtles

Wooden tools, especially those used for sourdough, absorb moisture over time. If left damp or in poorly ventilated spaces, they can develop mold or warp. This isn’t just about aesthetics—contaminants can affect your starter’s balance. A wooden spurtle stored improperly might also become brittle, risking splinters during use. The goal is to keep it dry, clean, and free from extreme temperature swings.

How to Store Your Wooden Spurtle for Sourdough Mixing

Follow these steps to preserve your tool:

  1. Dry it thoroughly after each use. After rinsing your spurtle with water (never submerge it for long), dry it immediately with a towel. Allow it to air dry further on a rack, but avoid direct sunlight or heat sources.
  2. Store in a cool, dry place. A drawer or kitchen cabinet away from your stove and sink is ideal. Avoid storing it in a plastic container unless you add a desiccant pack to reduce humidity.
  3. Keep it elevated. If you have a wooden spurtle and Danish dough whisk set, store the spurtle separately from the whisk. Lean it against a wall in a well-ventilated utensil rack or hang it with a hook.

Using the Right Tools for Sourdough Mixing

A quality set like the 2 PCS Wooden Spurtle Spatula for Sourdough Starter includes both a spurtle and a Danish dough whisk. These tools work together to mix, scrape jars, and blend starters efficiently. When stored properly, the spurtle’s ergonomic design won’t crack, and the whisk will stay splinter-free for years of use. Always let both tools dry completely before returning them to storage.

Maintenance Tips for Long-Lasting Wooden Spurtles

Even with proper storage, occasional upkeep is key. Once a month, apply a thin layer of food-safe mineral oil to condition the wood. Wipe it off after 15 minutes to prevent buildup. Inspect for cracks or mold regularly—early signs mean you can address issues before they compromise your tool’s integrity.

FAQ

Can I store my wooden spurtle in the same drawer as metal utensils?

Avoid storing wooden tools with metal ones in a damp drawer. Metal conducts moisture, increasing the risk of warping. Use separate compartments or a dedicated wooden utensil box.

Should I oil my spurtle if it’s stored dry?

Oiling isn’t needed if your spurtle is stored in a dry environment and shows no dryness or cracks. Over-oiling can attract dust and create residue over time.

Can a wooden spurtle be used for other kitchen tasks?

Yes! The Wooden Spurtle & Sourdough Starter Stirrer Set doubles as a jar scraper and mixing stick for batters, doughs, and even yogurt. Just avoid using it for acidic or salty ingredients long-term to prevent degradation.
